old_claim.note: Mechanical Refrigeration provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is scoped to successful clinical organ transplantation anchored by the 1954 living-donor kidney transplant; mechanical refrigeration and cold storage belong on deceased-donor preservation or scaling nodes.

source_shape.source_locator: Article body: the 1954 milestone was a kidney transplant between living identical twins, with rejection as the key barrier, not cold organ storage.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports the 1954 clinical-transplant claim and immunologic compatibility boundary without making refrigeration a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Refrigeration is relevant to later organ preservation logistics, but keeping it here smuggled deceased-donor preservation and cold-chain scaling into the first successful organ-transplant node.

ontology_before: The graph treated mechanical refrigeration as a direct prerequisite for organ transplantation generally.
ontology_after: The graph keeps cold storage out of the 1954 clinical transplant node and leaves it for preservation-specific or deceased-donor scaling nodes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-20-organ-transplantation-scope keeps refrigeration absent as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped specifically to deceased-donor organ preservation logistics.
A source showed mechanical refrigeration was directly required for the 1954 living-donor kidney transplant.
short_reason: Refrigeration is preservation/scaling context, not a direct prerequisite for the scoped 1954 clinical transplant achievement.
